{"step_by_step_thinking": "The question asks whether rotavirus vaccines have changed the predominant rotavirus genotypes. The provided documents discuss the impact of vaccines on disease burden, hospitalizations, seasonality, and the shift in leading causes of gastroenteritis (e.g., norovirus replacing rotavirus in high-income countries). However, none of the documents mention any change in the predominant rotavirus genotypes (such as shifts in genotype distribution due to vaccine use). The documents confirm a decline in rotavirus cases and a change in the leading cause of viral gastroenteritis in developed countries, but they do not provide evidence of a shift in the predominant genotypes of the virus.
